prompts: A SELECT regressed, but we do not yet know whether the bottleneck is plan estimates, blocking, I/O, or a missing index.

Add a new independently installable rust-unsafe-code-audit pack containing exactly one skill, rust-unsafe-boundary-review. Security reviewers need that focused installation and release boundary; rust-core-best-practices owns the nearest excluded general Rust review outcome. Research and integrate the complete pack.
Add exactly one skill named python-logging-boundary-review to the existing python-best-practices pack, using these detailed requirements and exclusions.
Create a comprehensive test matrix for command help, stdout, stderr, exit codes, stdin, and shell invocation.
Create a risk-based unit, integration, and end-to-end test plan for this existing Python package.
Design the user-facing diagnostic format, debug traceback policy, and numeric exit-status mapping for existing commands.
Given this diagnosed filter and sort pattern, specify the exact PostgreSQL index definition, rollout, and redundancy plan.
In an existing Rust CLI, add exactly one `project archive` command while preserving the discovered grammar, dispatch, output, and test conventions.
Keep polymorphism and public errors unchanged; replace this string tag and optional-field struct with data-carrying enums, group repeated parameters, and place behavior on natural owners.
Our async gRPC client reconnects for every call and retries timed-out writes; fix channel reuse and per-request deadline behavior.
Plan a low-lock production rollout for these already approved constraint and index changes on a 2 TB table.
Reconcile all Rust workspace READMEs, architecture documents, runbooks, and AGENTS.md files with current code; mention rustdoc drift separately.
Redesign every command in this Rust CLI around a new grammar, runtime context, configuration model, output protocol, and error taxonomy.
Redesign fixture scopes, coverage goals, markers, and the full Python project's pull-request test selection.
Refactor internal exceptions, context-manager cleanup, retries, and logging before any CLI mapping is considered.
Refresh crate-level and item-level rustdoc plus all doctestable examples across the workspace; report Markdown drift separately without editing Markdown.
Refresh the public API prose, repair intra-doc links, and rewrite its examples so readers understand safety, errors, and platform behavior.
Review a Python command's messages, debug mode, stderr routing, and process exit status when exceptions occur.
Review these existing CREATE TABLE statements and catalog snapshots, rank defects, and propose minimal corrections.
Several HTTP and database background tasks ignore cancellation and prevent the Tokio process from shutting down; redesign their shared task lifecycle.
Split this Rust application into a library, two binaries, and workspace packages, deciding module ownership, dependency direction, and integration-test placement.
Split this Rust application into a thin binary and several library crates, deciding which dependencies each new boundary should own.
The type layout is already stable; choose whether three implementations should use generics, a trait object, or closed enum and define lifetimes and typed public errors.
We know the query is slow but have not chosen the exact access method, columns, order, or partial predicate.
We need an operational rollback and restore trigger for one approved high-risk schema migration next week.
Within the existing module tree, choose between a generic, trait object, and closed enum for three runtime-selectable implementations and design their error contract.
